headlines.title: Protesters in Serbia doubt Vucic resignation will bring change
headlines.snippet: Thousands gathered in Serbia's Kraljevo, dismissing Aleksandar Vucic's pledge to resign.

headlines.title: Pope Leo prays for Venezuela’s recovery after deadly quakes
headlines.snippet: Pope Leo expressed solidarity with Venezuelans following two deadly earthquakes that resulted in at least 1,400 fatalities.

headlines.title: Israel kills four Palestinians in Gaza as attacks on tents continue
headlines.snippet: Israeli forces restricted Palestinian worshippers at Al-Aqsa Mosque and blocked the call to prayer at the Ibrahimi Mosque, while also killing four Palestinians in Gaza.

headlines.title: Hope of finding more Venezuela quake survivors fades despite late rescues
headlines.snippet: Hope for finding more survivors of the Venezuela earthquakes is diminishing, despite some individuals, including a 60-year-old woman trapped for 86 hours, being rescued. Tens of thousands are still reported missing.

headlines.title: Gaza’s displaced families face worsening living conditions
headlines.snippet: Displaced families in Gaza are enduring worsening living conditions, with rats infesting camps and widespread disease due to uncollected waste and a lack of sanitation.

headlines.title: Israel shells Syrian village near occupied Golan Heights
headlines.snippet: Israel has shelled a Syrian village in the western countryside of Deraa province, near the occupied Golan Heights, according to Syria’s official news agency SANA. Israeli military aircraft were also reported flying over areas of Quneitra and Deraa.

headlines.title: ‘Financial death penalty’: How US sanctions are upending the lives of ICC judges
headlines.snippet: Three International Criminal Court (ICC) judges have faced severe financial repercussions, including frozen bank accounts and loss of essential services, due to sanctions imposed by the Trump administration. These measures, enacted under Executive Order 14203, impact their personal lives and freedom of movement.

headlines.title: Gaza dialysis patients at risk as machines fail
headlines.snippet: Nearly half of the dialysis machines at Gaza’s largest hospital are non-operational due to Israel’s blockade and attacks, crippling the health system. A shortage of essential substances has taken 25 machines out of service, with the remaining 26 operating partially.

headlines.title: Israeli forces push deeper into southern Syria
headlines.snippet: Israeli forces have advanced further into Syria’s Deraa province, establishing new checkpoints and deploying additional military vehicles. This expansion includes setting up checkpoints in the villages of Abidin and near Sreyeh Jamlah.

headlines.title: Qatari citizen killed by shrapnel after vessel goes missing
headlines.snippet: A Qatari citizen has died from shrapnel injuries sustained during "military operations in the area," according to Qatar’s Ministry of Interior. Search teams located a missing vessel, confirming the citizen's death and reporting another individual wounded and hospitalized.
